question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 PermitTransferFrom for exactly maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where all offers partially fill (filledBuyerAssets < maxBuyerAssets) trigger pullToken consuming the full maxBuyerAssets nonce then have the refund SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets) revert due to a token-level blacklist or pause, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen outside valid protocol rules, causing scoped impact: user loses maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets tokens permanently locked in MidnightBundles? Proof idea: deploy a loanToken that blacklists msg.sender after the Permit2 transfer succeeds; assert that the refund path reverts and the bundler retains the difference.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged BORROWER who signs a Permit2 permit for assets under PRECONDITIONS where referralFeePct = WAD - 1 trigger pullToken consuming assets then have units = assets - referralFeeAssets = 1 repaid to Midnight while referralFeeAssets = assets - 1 is sent to referralFeeRecipient, violating the invariant that user funds must not be seized outside valid protocol rules, causing scoped impact: borrower loses nearly all repay tokens to the referral fee recipient with only 1 unit of debt cleared? Proof idea: fuzz assets and referralFeePct near WAD - 1; assert borrower net debt reduction vs tokens spent ratio.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken (PermitKind.Permit2 branch) vs pullToken (PermitKind.ERC2612 branch)] Can an unprivileged FRONT-RUNNER who observes a pending buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral tx with PermitKind.ERC2612 under PRECONDITIONS where the permit has not yet been consumed trigger IERC20Permit.permit front-run then have the bundler silently continue via try-catch and succeed via pre-existing allowance, while the same attack against PermitKind.Permit2 causes the bundler tx to revert with InvalidNonce, violating the invariant that equivalent user flows must not have asymmetric DoS exposure, causing scoped impact: Permit2 users face nonce-based DoS that ERC2612 users do not? Proof idea: differential test both permit kinds under front-run; assert ERC2612 succeeds and Permit2 reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where the loanToken is a fee-on-transfer token trigger pullToken transferring maxBuyerAssets from msg.sender but the bundler receiving maxBuyerAssets - fee, then have forceApproveMax approve Midnight for type(uint256).max, then have Midnight's take pull buyerAssets from the bundler, violating the invariant that total credit and token balances must stay solvent, causing scoped impact: bundler may lack sufficient tokens to cover buyerAssets causing take to revert after Permit2 nonce is consumed? Proof idea: deploy a fee-on-transfer loanToken; assert that the Permit2 nonce is consumed but the take reverts, leaving the user unable to retry with the same nonce.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged BORROWER who signs a Permit2 permit for collateral assets under PRECONDITIONS where the collateral token is a fee-on-transfer token trigger pullToken consuming the Permit2 nonce and transferring collateralSupplies[i].assets from msg.sender but the bundler receiving less, then have supplyCollateral credit the full collateralSupplies[i].assets to taker on Midnight while Midnight's safeTransferFrom pulls only the bundler's actual balance, violating the invariant that collateral accounting must match actual token balances, causing scoped impact: Midnight's collateral balance is overcredited relative to actual token holdings? Proof idea: deploy a fee-on-transfer collateral token; assert midnight.collateral(id, taker, i) > actual token balance of Midnight.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) overflows or exceeds maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets trigger the refund subtraction maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets to underflow and revert, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: the Permit2 nonce is consumed (transfer succeeded) but the whole tx reverts, leaving the user unable to use that nonce again and requiring a new signature? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct near WAD - 1 and filledBuyerAssets near maxBuyerAssets; assert underflow revert and nonce state.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for targetBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where all offers revert (caught by try-catch) trigger pullToken consuming targetBuyerAssets from msg.sender then have require(filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets, OutOfOffers()) revert, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: the Permit2 nonce is consumed but the tx reverts, so the user must sign a new permit to retry? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged BORROWER who signs a Permit2 permit for assets under PRECONDITIONS where units = assets - referralFeeAssets > position[id][onBehalf].debt trigger pullToken consuming the Permit2 nonce then have Midnight's repay revert due to debt underflow (position.debt -= toUint128(units) reverts), violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: the Permit2 nonce is consumed but the tx reverts, forcing the user to sign a new permit to retry repayment? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where the loanToken is a rebasing token that increases balances between pullToken and the refund safeTransfer trigger the bundler to hold more tokens than maxBuyerAssets after the rebase, violating the invariant that total token balances must stay solvent, causing scoped impact: the bundler retains the rebased excess tokens that belong to the user? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where the loanToken is a rebasing token that decreases balances between pullToken and Midnight's take trigger Midnight's safeTransferFrom(loanToken, bundler, midnight, buyerAssets - sellerAssets) to revert because the bundler's balance dropped below buyerAssets - sellerAssets, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: the Permit2 nonce is consumed but the tx reverts, leaving the user unable to retry with the same nonce? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: forceApproveMax / pullToken] Can an unprivileged TAKER who calls buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with PermitKind.Permit2 under PRECONDITIONS where the bundler's existing allowance to Midnight is exactly type(uint96).max / 2 trigger forceApproveMax to skip re-approval, then have Midnight's take decrement the allowance below the needed buyerAssets amount, violating the invariant that every borrow/take must create correct debt and collateral obligation, causing scoped impact: Midnight's safeTransferFrom reverts due to insufficient allowance, blocking the take? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged BORROWER who signs Permit2 permits for multiple CollateralSupply entries under PRECONDITIONS where the second collateral's Permit2 nonce is already consumed trigger the first pullToken to succeed (nonce consumed, tokens transferred) then the second pullToken to revert (InvalidNonce), violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: the first collateral's Permit2 nonce is consumed but the tx reverts, so the user must sign a new permit for the first collateral to retry? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where msg.sender != taker (delegated call) trigger pullToken pulling from msg.sender (not taker) via Permit2, then have the take credit taker on Midnight, violating the invariant that user funds must not be stolen, causing scoped impact: an authorized operator can drain msg.sender's tokens via Permit2 while crediting a different taker address? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged BORROWER who signs a Permit2 permit for assets under PRECONDITIONS where msg.sender != onBehalf (delegated repay) trigger pullToken pulling from msg.sender via Permit2, then have Midnight repay onBehalf's debt, then have withdrawCollateral send collateral to collateralReceiver, violating the invariant that user funds must not be stolen, causing scoped impact: an authorized operator can use their own Permit2 signature to repay another user's debt and withdraw that user's collateral to an arbitrary receiver? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where the IPermit2 interface at PERMIT2 = 0x000000000022D473030F116dDEE9F6B43aC78BA3 is not deployed (e.g., on a new chain or testnet) trigger pullToken to call a non-existent contract and silently succeed (no code check in pullToken for Permit2), violating the invariant that user funds must not be stolen, causing scoped impact: the Permit2 call succeeds as a no-op, the bundler has no tokens, and Midnight's take reverts, but the user believes the tx succeeded? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where the loanToken address in the Permit2 signature differs from takes[0].offer.market.loanToken trigger IPermit2.permitTransferFrom to transfer a different token than the bundler expects, violating the invariant that total credit and token balances must stay solvent, causing scoped impact: the bundler receives the wrong token, forceApproveMax approves the wrong token to Midnight, and Midnight's take pulls the correct loanToken from the bundler's pre-existing balance? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where the Permit2 signature encodes a requestedAmount less than maxBuyerAssets (i.e., TokenPermissions.amount < maxBuyerAssets) trigger IPermit2.permitTransferFrom to revert with InvalidAmount, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: the user's tx reverts because the bundler always sets requestedAmount = amount = maxBuyerAssets but the signature only covers a smaller amount? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where the Permit2 nonce bitmap word (nonce >> 8) is at a position that causes _useUnorderedNonce to flip the wrong bit trigger a nonce collision with a different nonce, violating the invariant that offers must not be replayed, causing scoped impact: two different Permit2 nonces map to the same bitmap position, allowing one to invalidate the other? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged BORROWER who signs a Permit2 permit for assets under PRECONDITIONS where referralFeeAssets = assets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD) rounds down by 1 wei trigger units = assets - referralFeeAssets to be 1 wei more than intended, causing Midnight's repay to pull 1 extra wei from the bundler, violating the invariant that total credit, debt, and token balances must stay solvent, causing scoped impact: the bundler retains 0 tokens but the referralFeeRecipient receives 1 wei less than expected, creating a 1-wei accounting discrepancy? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: pullToken / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged TAKER who signs a Permit2 permit for maxBuyerAssets under PRECONDITIONS where the Permit2 DOMAIN_SEPARATOR is cached for a different chainId (e.g., after a chain fork) trigger IPermit2.permitTransferFrom to accept a signature intended for a different chain, violating the invariant that signatures must not be bypassable, causing scoped impact: a signature from chain A is replayed on chain B, allowing unauthorized token transfers?
